options.description: Detects variables in current or provided text, and asks for the values of the detected variables, which can be used in a replacement token in the format {VARIABLE_NAME}.
The config file to be used. To generate a blank config file, use the --generateConfig option with this option, and a blank config file will be generated in this path.
Automatically cycle the texts, pausing for the specified interval.
If defined, waits for the specified number of seconds after setting the target file. Useful for letting the result message be read.
Overrides the initial time for the time replacement tokens and countdowns.
Displays a menu for selecting the desired text.
Defines an arbitrary value for a sequence. Specify the sequence index and the value, separated by a comma.
Overrides the 'targetFile' setting in the config file with a path to a file in which the next text will be written to.
Defines index of the text to be set on the output file. If not provided, TextCycler will cicle through the texts provided in the config file.

Asks for the value of a variable, which can be used in a replacement token in the format {VARIABLE_NAME}. Optionally, you can provide a default value to the variable, which will be displayed at prompt, using a colon after the variable name, and providing the value after that colon.

Sets the value of a variable, which can be used in a replacement token in the format {VARIABLE_NAME}. You must provide the value for the variable using a colon after the variable name, and providing the value after that colon.
